qa.overall_assessment: Iteration 5 adds the single missing AC10 check that was the sole blocker in iter4. ISSUE-NEW-1 (AC10 THEN asserted /root/ form absent but check object provided no recipe to verify it) is now resolved: two new flat keys — old_root_form_absent_verification_command and old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1 — close the gap. The verification command is `grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md` with expected exit code 1 (PASS = absent). Codex adversarial review confirmed the approach is correct and proposed three shape improvements (flat keys instead of nested object, direct grep + exit_code instead of compound semicolon expression, path-only pattern instead of python3-prefixed pattern), all of which were incorporated. AC1-AC9 and the three pre-existing AC10 checks are unchanged. All prior passing checks from iter4 remain in effect.

issue_resolution.resolution_description: Added two flat keys to AC10 check object: 'old_root_form_absent_verification_command': 'grep -F /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py commands/push.md' and 'old_root_form_absent_exit_code': 1. Also updated AC10 when field to include the new grep command and updated AC10 then field to state the fourth outcome (exits code 1, old form absent). Self-verification: on pre-dev commands/push.md (line 183 contains old form), grep exits 0 — check FAILS as expected. On simulated post-dev file (tilde form only), grep exits 1 — check PASSES as expected.

self_verification_evidence.pre_dev_state: grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md → exit 0, match at line 183: 'python3 /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --request-id ...' — FAIL state correctly detected (old form still present)
self_verification_evidence.post_dev_simulation: printf 'python3 ~/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py --branch ...' | grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' → exit 1 — PASS state correctly detected (old form absent)
self_verification_evidence.fail_closed_on_error: grep exits 2 if commands/push.md does not exist; exit 2 != 1, so check FAILS closed (does not false-pass on infrastructure errors)

codex_consult.feedback_summary: Codex raised 4 findings against the originally proposed nested-sub-object shape with compound semicolon expression. All 4 findings were in scope and had PROPOSED_FIX entries.

findings.issue: grep -qF ...; [ $? -ne 0 ] exits 0 on grep error (e.g. missing file, grep exit 2) — false pass on infrastructure failure
findings.proposed_fix: Use flat keys old_root_form_absent_verification_command and old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1; run grep directly; compare exit code to 1 (not 'not 0')
findings.rationale: Direct grep + expected exit_code: 1 fails closed when grep exits 2 (file missing or permission error). The compound expression ; [ $? -ne 0 ] would pass on exit 2 — a silent false pass in infrastructure error conditions.

findings.issue: Nested sub-object shape { 'old_absolute_form_absent': { description, verification_command, expected_exit_code } } inconsistent with AC10's existing flat key style (*_verification_command, *_exit_code)
findings.proposed_fix: Use flat keys: old_root_form_absent_verification_command (string) and old_root_form_absent_exit_code (integer 1)
findings.rationale: Flat keys match the existing AC10 check object style used for invocation_args_verification_command and old_and_pattern_absent_exit_code. Nested object would require QA to parse a different schema shape for this one check.

findings.issue: Pattern python3 /root/.claude/... is slightly too narrow — only catches invocations with exactly 'python3 ' prefix; any alternative absolute invocation form would escape
findings.proposed_fix: Grep for the path alone: grep -F '/root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py' commands/push.md
findings.rationale: Matching the path without the python3 prefix catches any absolute invocation form. This is strictly more comprehensive than the python3-prefixed form and is consistent with what iter4's proposed_fix used in its shorter notation.

findings.issue: AC10 when field should describe raw commands run, not include inverted pass logic; then field should state exit code 1 outcome explicitly
findings.proposed_fix: Update when to include 'grep -F /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py commands/push.md is run'; update then to say it exits with code 1
findings.rationale: The when field is a narrative of what operations are performed; the then field states the expected outcomes including exit codes. Inversion logic belongs in the then clause, not the when clause.

codex_consult.feedback_incorporated: All 4 Codex findings applied. Final AC10 check shape uses flat keys, direct grep with path-only pattern, expected exit_code: 1, and updated when/then text.

unchanged_checks.note: All acceptance criteria AC1 through AC9 are byte-for-byte identical to iter4. No modifications made.

The three pre-existing AC10 check keys (invocation_uses_tilde_form, invocation_contains_all_args with invocation_args_verification_command / invocation_args_must_contain, old_and_pattern_absent_exit_code, grep_check) are unchanged from iter4.

ba_validation_dimensions.evidence_quality: pass — all claims backed by live greps. Pre-dev state confirmed: grep exit 0 at commands/push.md line 183. Post-dev simulation confirmed: grep exit 1 when old form absent. Fail-closed behavior on infrastructure errors confirmed by exit code analysis (grep exit 2 != 1).
ba_validation_dimensions.scope_alignment: pass — only AC10 check object modified. Two flat keys added, ac_uid bumped to v5, iteration bumped to 5, notes field updated. No other ACs, no MoSCoW items, no Technical Hints changed.
ba_validation_dimensions.spec_text_vs_execution_drift: pass — the new AC10 check object now contains a mechanically executable recipe for every assertion in the THEN text: (1) tilde form present: grep_check; (2) all args present: invocation_args_verification_command + invocation_args_must_contain; (3) old && patterns absent: old_and_pattern_absent_exit_code; (4) old /root/ form absent: old_root_form_absent_verification_command + old_root_form_absent_exit_code. All four THEN assertions are now mechanically verifiable.
ba_validation_dimensions.investigation_completeness: pass — same as iter4 (no new investigation scope added; this is a targeted AC recipe correction only).

files_modified.changes: iteration: 4 → 5; notes updated; AC10 when field extended with new grep command; AC10 then field extended with fourth outcome clause; AC10 check object gains old_root_form_absent_verification_command and old_root_form_absent_exit_code: 1; ac_uid bumped to ac10-push-md-tilde-form-branch-arg-v5

AC10 section gains fourth bullet: 'AND grep -F /root/.claude/scripts/execute-push.py commands/push.md exits with code 1 (old /root/.claude/ absolute execute-push path absent — M13 requires replacement, not addition alongside)'

qa.approval_notes: APPROVED — iteration 5 resolves the sole blocking issue from iter4 (ISSUE-NEW-1: AC10 check object provided no recipe to verify the /root/ absolute form is absent). The fix is minimal: two new flat keys in the AC10 check object plus corresponding when/then text updates. Codex adversarial review confirmed the fix is correct and proposed shape improvements (all applied). All other ACs unchanged. Dev may proceed with implementation.
